mods.note: See Sharf 2002. Attributed to Sengzhao, but rather a work associated with the Ox Head lineage of Chan. Shares terminology with the Treatise on the Transcendence of Cognition 絕觀論 (only extant in Dunhuang manuscripts), traditionally attributed to the first Ox Head patriarch, Niutou Farong 牛頭法融 (594-657), but probably a work of a later Ox Head figure working in the last quarter of the eighth century. Sharf, 2002, 43: More recent studies by Yanagida and McRae suggest that the Chüeh-kuan lun may have been compiled by a later member of the Ox Head School and may date to the third quarter of the eighth century. This dating would make the text roughly contemporaneous with the Treasure Store Treatise, rendering the direction of influence (if the influence was direct at all) impossible to determine. p. 46: ...the Treasure Store Treatise and the Chüeh-kuan lun hold similar positions with respect to nien-fo, and both advert to the same passage from the Vimalakīrti-sūtra for scriptural support.

titleInfo.title: New edition of the Buddhist canon in the Taishō era
